title: [CI:DOCS] Remove disused Containerfile and docs
body:
Fixes: #21574

The documentation for this image references a quay repository that doesn't exist.  It doesn't appear any of these files have been touched since late 2022, let's just remove them.

Users needing to access remote podman can simply use the `podman` binary present in existing images `quay.io/containers/podman`, `quay.io/podman/stable`, etc.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
> Users needing to access remote podman can simply use the podman binary present in existing images quay.io/containers/podman, quay.io/podman/stable, etc.

This is not an alternative as they are not static remote binaries and don't help for windows/macos at all. 

Either way my best guess looking at the git history is that this was added here but no actual builds were ever made so removing this shouldn't hurt but maybe @praveenkumar and @cfergeau should comment here if they use it somewhere since they added/modifed these files.

Thanks Paul.  That was my interpretation of the history as well.  For the record, I'm not opposed to adding an automated build for this.  But the Containerfile will need quite a bit of work to shape it into something the current build system will accept (and be performant).  I am planning on touching this automation soon, so if that's the direction we want to go I can add it into my planning.
Marking as a draft to prevent merging, in case further discussion is needed.
I think we're good to go ahead and remove these files.  The original reporter has found a suitable solution for their needs.
